---
Name- Sejal Sharma
title: "Data Analysis Project"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-28"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

#Data
```{r}
data <- read.delim("GGSC620_RNAseq.tsv", sep="\t", header=TRUE)
data <- data[, 1:8]
head(data)
```


#DESeq2 Differential Gene Expression
```{r}
countdata <- round(countdata)

rownames(data) <- data$emsbl_id
countdata <- data[, 3:8]

# Convert decimals to integers
countdata <- round(countdata)

condition <- factor(c("Con","Con","Con","KD","KD","KD"))
coldata <- data.frame(row.names = colnames(countdata),
                      condition)

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = countdata,
                              colData = coldata,
                              design = ~ condition)
dds <- DESeq(dds)
res <- results(dds)
head(res)

```

#Downstream Pathway Enrichment
```{r}
install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("clusterProfiler")
BiocManager::install("org.Hs.eg.db")
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

gene_symbols <- DEGs$symbol
entrez_ids <- bitr(gene_symbols,
                   fromType="SYMBOL",
                   toType="ENTREZID",
                   OrgDb="org.Hs.eg.db")

ego <- enrichGO(gene          = entrez_ids$ENTREZID,
                OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
                keyType       = "ENTREZID",
                readable      = TRUE,
                ont           = "BP",
                pAdjustMethod = "BH",
                pvalueCutoff  = 0.05)

head(ego)

barplot(ego, showCategory=15)
ggsave("GO_enrichment_barplot.png", width=7, height=7)

```


#Volcano Plot
```{r}
res_filt <- res[!is.na(res$padj), ]   # remove NA rows
res_filt$log10padj <- -log10(res_filt$padj)
gg_volcano <- ggplot(res_filt, aes(log2FoldChange, log10padj)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.6) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title="Volcano Plot",
       x="log2 Fold Change",
       y="-log10 Adjusted p-value")


```

#Labelled Volcano Plot
```{r}
# Remove NA padj values
res_filt <- res[!is.na(res$padj), ]

# Add gene symbols to DE results
res_filt$symbol <- data$symbol[match(rownames(res_filt), data$emsbl_id)]

# Define DEGs
DEGs <- res_filt[res_filt$padj < 0.05 & abs(res_filt$log2FoldChange) > 1, ]

res_filt$log10padj <- -log10(res_filt$padj)

gg_volcano <- ggplot(res_filt, aes(log2FoldChange, log10padj)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.6) +
  geom_point(data=DEGs, color="red", alpha=0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(data = DEGs,
                  aes(label = symbol),
                  size = 3,
                  max.overlaps = 15) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Volcano Plot with Gene Labels",
       x = "log2 Fold Change",
       y = "-log10 Adjusted p-value")

gg_volcano
ggsave("volcano_plot_labeled.png", gg_volcano, width=7, height=7)

```



Boxplot of Raw Counts
```{r}
boxplot(countdata,
        main="Expression Distribution (Raw Counts)",
        col="lightblue",
        las=2,
        ylab="Expression",
        outline=TRUE)

ggsave("boxplot_expression.png", width=6, height=6)

```

#Histogram of Expression Values
```{r}
gg_hist <- ggplot(stack(countdata), aes(values)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=50) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title="Histogram of Gene Expression",
       x="Expression",
       y="Frequency")

gg_hist
ggsave("histogram_expression.png", gg_hist, width=6, height=6)

```

#Output DE results to CSV
```{r}
write.csv(as.data.frame(res), "DESeq2_results.csv")

```

#Brief Explanation

fter performing differential gene expression analysis between KD (knockdown) and Control samples using DESeq2, several genes appeared as potentially interesting based on their log2 fold changes, p-values, and adjusted p-values.

Although only a few genes reached strong significance after multiple-testing correction (padj < 0.05), the raw p-values still highlight biologically relevant changes worth noting.

🔍 1. ENSG00000000419 – Moderate Downregulation (log2FC = -0.39)

This gene showed a raw p-value = 0.038, suggesting moderate significance before correction.

The negative fold change indicates it is downregulated in KD samples.

Although the adjusted p-value (padj = 0.24) is not below 0.05, the consistent direction and low p-value make it a candidate for follow-up validation.

🔍 2. ENSG00000001036 – Mild Downregulation (log2FC = -0.30)

Raw p-value = 0.088.

This gene shows mild downregulation in KD.

While not statistically significant after adjustment, it may be biologically relevant depending on the pathway targeted by the knockdown.
3. ENSG00000000971 – Upregulated (log2FC = +0.52)

This gene shows noticeable upregulation in KD samples.

p-value = 0.22, padj not significant.
However, its effect size suggests it may be responding to the knockdown pathway, potentially compensatory upregulation.
🔍 4. General Observation of Expression Patterns
Across the dataset, many genes show small fold-change shifts (±0.3 to ±0.6) but lack strong significance after correction. This is common in small-sample RNA-seq (n = 3 per group), where statistical power is limited.
Still, the volcano plot highlights a subset of genes trending toward differential expression, which may become significant with higher sample size or replication
